title: Camp Sagol
content:
{{Top|ga|leg}} {{Location |image=[[File:CampSagol.jpg]] |name=Camp Sagol |created=Between [[0 ABY/Legends|0.5 ABY]] and [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]]''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' places the mission's events during that time period. |destroyed= |type=[[Imperial Military/Legends|Military]] camp''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]'' |location=[[Krant]] |terrain= |fauna= |flora= |interest= |affiliation=[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] }} {{Quote|The Empire has set up camps to the south and far east. They are preparing for an attack on the peaceful villages of Peche and Theenes.''"
"''We'll need to take at least one out.|[[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] and Princess Leia on the Imperial camps|}} '''Camp Sagol''' was an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] military camp situated on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Krant]] and constructed after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]] in [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]]. During the [[Battle of Krant (Galactic Civil War)|Battle of Krant]] between the Empire and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], the Imperial forces at Camp Sagol threatened the [[Krantian/Legends|Krantian]] [[City/Legends|cities]] of [[Peche]] and [[Theenes]]. ==Description== Camp Sagol was an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Imperial Military/Legends|military]] camp established on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Krant]] between [[0 ABY/Legends|0.5]] and [[2 ABY/Legends|2]] [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]. It was located southwest of the [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Peche]]. Camp Sagol was inside a set of walls, with part of the left side abutting a cliff, and the base consisted of a [[Command center|Command Center]], three [[Barracks|troop centers]], a [[Factory/Legends|mech factory]], and eight [[Prefab shelter]]s along with a [[War Center]] and [[spaceport]]. A group of [[ASP-7 labor droid/Legends|ASP-7 labor droids]] were at its disposal for resource-gathering, and four [[Power core/Legends|power cores]] provided power to the base's buildings. Two [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser turrets]] at the entrance protected the camp, and a network of additional laser turrets was spread throughout the area. The base also had a [[Garrison/Legends|garrison]] of [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]]. ==History== After the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] [[Diplomat/Legends|emissary]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] crashed on the planet, a [[Squad/Legends|squad]] of Camp Sagol's [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] attacked a small [[Krantian/Legends|Krantian]] [[Small Village|village]], although they were [[Death/Legends|cut down]] by Organa's [[Rebel trooper/Legends|escorts]] and the exiled [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Echuu Shen-Jon]]. Camp Sagol, along with another Imperial camp, [[Camp Kaxol|Kaxol]], [[Battle of Krant (Galactic Civil War)|commenced their attacks]] on the Krantian cities of Peche and [[Theenes]], targeting the [[bridge]]s which connected the cities to each other. The two military camps sent a group of [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] and [[Dewback trooper/Legends|dewback troopers]]. Organa took control of the small village and raised an [[Army/Legends|army]] to defeat Sagol and Kaxol, destroying their ability to make war. ==Behind the scenes== {{Quote|You have beaten Camp Sagol!!!|The notification to the player after defeating Camp Sagol|}} Camp Sagol first appeared in the [[2001]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]''. One of the two camps must be destroyed by the player's army, forcing the other camp to withdraw. It has not been [[canon]]ically established which was destroyed, as the player can complete the level by attacking and eliminating either one or the other. As the choice is a matter of game mechanics, this article is neutral to that choice. Only one of the camps has to be destroyed to finish the mission, although the game's [[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|strategy guide]], released before the game itself, suggests the player target Kaxol, the closer camp to the Krantian cities.[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]] ==Appearances== *''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]'' {{1st}} *''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Clone Campaigns]]'' ==Sources== *[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]] {{1stm}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Lager Sagol#legends }} [[Category:Imperial military bases]] [[Category:Krant locations]]
